# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Docker Compose file for ELK Stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) and APM Server. Configure all services with network settings. Set up communication between components.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent monitoring misconfiguration.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: monitoring_misconfiguration


version: '3'  # Define the version of docker compose file format (v3)
services:   # Start defining services here
    elk_stack:     # Name your service and define it as a new one in Docker Compose. This is where you configure all ELK Stack components like Elasticsearch, Logstash & Kibana – the network settings are already set up by default (as per docker-compose file).
        image: elkstack/elk   # Use official images for simplicity and efficiency in development environment. For production environments use your own Docker Image URLs or build from source code if necessary, e.g., 'docker build -t my_custom_image .' – replace with the appropriate command based on requirements
        networks:    # Define network settings here (default is created by docker-compose) for communication between components in ELK Stack and APM Server are already set up as per default configuration. If you want to change them, use this section accordingly e.g., 'networks:' -> elk_network
            myapp:   # Name your network (myapp), define it here if needed for communication between components in ELK Stack and APM Server – replace with the appropriate name based on requirements or needs of services within same networks, ie e.g., 'networks:' -> elk_network
                driver: bridge   # Use this network type (bridge) by default as per docker-compose file configuration for communication between components in ELK Stack and APM Server – replace with the appropriate command based on requirements or needs of services within same networks, ie e.g., 'networks:' -> elk_network

            image: docker.elastic.co/apm/server   # Use official images for simplicity and efficiency in development environment as per requirement (Elastic Cloud APM Server) – replace with the appropriate command based on requirements or needs of services within same networks, ie e.g., 'networks:' -> elk_network

            restart: unless-stopped   # Restart service if it stops due to network issues by default in docker compose file configuration for communication between components (ELK Stack and APM Server) – replace with the appropriate command based on requirements or needs of services within same networks, ie e.g., 'restart:' -> elk
